options.prompt:
[legacy-chat-summary-atlas]

Produce an operator-grade feature atlas for Script Kit GPUI feature 031, Legacy `chat()` Prompt. Return text only. Do not create/download/attach files.

Project: Rust/GPUI desktop runtime plus TypeScript SDK. Feature map chapters must help humans and AI agents fully understand capabilities, states, interactions, script APIs, runtime routing, tests, receipts, and gaps. `lat check` is required after local docs work.

Scope: legacy SDK `chat()` conversational prompt surface, distinct from ACP `ai*` APIs. Include SDK entry, protocol messages, Rust ChatPrompt UI, callback mode, built-in AI mode, setup mode, actions dialog, inline Mini AI reuse, persistence, handoff, keyboard/mouse interactions, tests, observability, gaps.

Source-derived evidence from local inspection:
- `scripts/kit-sdk.ts` defines `chat()`. It creates a unique id, stores `currentChatId`, sets `currentConversationId = conv-${id}`, normalizes initial messages, prepends `system` shorthand, and emits `{ type: "chat", id, placeholder, messages, hint, footer, actions, model, models, saveHistory, useBuiltinAi }`.
- SDK `useBuiltinAi` is true only when neither `onInit` nor `onMessage` is supplied. If `onInit` exists, SDK awaits it after opening prompt. If `onMessage` exists, app-to-SDK `chatSubmit` is routed to script callback; SDK appends user message locally, awaits `onMessage`, and keeps listening.
- SDK controller helpers require `currentChatId`: `chat.addMessage`, `chat.startStream`, `chat.appendChunk`, `chat.completeStream`, `chat.clear`, `chat.setError`, `chat.clearError`, `chat.getMessages`, `chat.getResult`. They send `chatMessage`, `chatStreamStart`, `chatStreamChunk`, `chatStreamComplete`, `chatClear`, `chatSetError`, and `chatClearError`.
- Rust protocol defines `Message::Chat`, `ChatAddMessage`, `ChatStreamStart`, `ChatStreamChunk`, `ChatStreamComplete`, `ChatClear`, `ChatSetError`, `ChatClearError`, and app-to-SDK `ChatSubmit`. `ChatPromptMessage` has role/position/content/text/id/model/streaming/error/created_at/image behavior.
- `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs` creates `ChatPrompt`, wires `ChatSubmitCallback` to send `Message::ChatSubmit { id, text }`, applies models/default model/save history, sets `current_view = AppView::ChatPrompt { id, entity }`, focus target `ChatPrompt`, and resizes to compact AI view for mode.
- In `use_builtin_ai`, prompt handler builds provider registry. Providers present: `with_builtin_ai`, maybe `needs_initial_response`. No providers: `needs_setup`, configure and Claude Code callbacks.
- Chat prompt control messages are accepted only when current view is `AppView::ChatPrompt` and ids match; mismatches are ignored.
- `ChatPrompt` state includes messages, placeholder, hint, footer, title, input, callbacks, save_history, provider registry, selected model/models, built-in streaming state, pending submit, setup state, action callbacks, image/paste state, scroll/list state, mini mode.
- Submit flow: empty input without image ignored; pasted text tokens expanded. Callback mode invokes SDK submit callback. Built-in AI mode blocks concurrent streaming, expands context mentions, applies slash command transforms, adds user message, creates empty streaming assistant message, chooses provider/model, and spawns streaming reveal. Missing provider/model creates assistant error messages.
- State flow: turn cache, scroll-follow, add message, stream start/chunk/complete, clear, `is_streaming`, `stop_streaming`. Escape/Cmd+. stops streaming and preserves partial content; otherwise Escape closes.
- Keyboard: Escape, Cmd+., End/Cmd+Down, Cmd+K, Cmd+Enter, Enter, Shift+Enter, Cmd+C, Cmd+Backspace, Cmd+V, text input delegation. Setup mode maps Tab/Shift+Tab/Up/Down/Enter/Space/Escape across Configure and Claude Code buttons.
- `actions.rs` saves non-empty conversations to AI DB as `ChatSource::ChatPrompt` when closing inline with `saveHistory`; skips inline save when transferring to AI window and opens AI window with pending messages. Also copy last response, clear conversation, script-generation save/run status, and delegates actions popup to parent.
- `render_prompts/other.rs` renders ChatPrompt through shared simple shell with no outer footer because chat owns its footer. Parent shell intercepts Cmd+K and routes actions dialog keys for `ActionsDialogHost::ChatPrompt`.
- Parent actions: `ActionsDialog::with_chat` exposes model selection and chat actions. `execute_chat_action` handles `select_model_*`, `continue_in_chat`, `expand_full_chat`, `copy_response`, destructive-confirm `clear_conversation`, and `capture_screen_area`.
- Inline Mini AI reuse: `prompt_ai.rs` opens `inline-ai-setup` setup card if no providers; otherwise opens `inline-ai` with built-in AI, optional initial query/pending submit, save history on, and typed `MiniAiUiRequest::ToggleActions` channel.
- `render_impl.rs` drains inline chat channels: escape captures Mini AI close snapshot and returns main menu; actions dispatch through parent window; continue hides/removes main window for AI handoff; configure opens API key prompt; Claude Code enables config.
- Tests: SDK chat test; smoke chat prompt/onInit/callback/error/visual tests; Rust contracts for Mini AI close snapshots, sizing, and actions routing.

Deliverable shape:
1. Capability summary and ACP boundary.
2. Entry points and activation paths.
3. User stories.
4. State model.
5. Interaction contract.
6. SDK/protocol contract.
7. Persistence and side effects.
8. Agent/API observability.
9. Boundaries/non-goals.
10. Verification map.
11. Risks/gaps.
12. A concise maintained-chapter draft for `feature-map/features/031-legacy-chat-prompt.md`.

Be comprehensive and practical. Mark inferred behavior clearly.
